title: Marketplace validation fails: unrecognized `commandWindows` field in hooks.json breaks install via Claude.ai plugin marketplace
author: timescorporate
body:
When adding this repo as a plugin marketplace in Claude.ai (Cowork), installation fails with:

```
MARKETPLACE_ERROR:REMOTE_SYNC_FAILED ‚Äî status: failed_content
"1 plugins found, 1 failed validation"
plugin "ponytail": Unknown hook field(s) ['commandWindows'] in 'SessionStart' and 'UserPromptSubmit' hook 0.
Hooks must only use fields the approval UI can display.
```

Looking at `hooks/hooks.json`, both the `SessionStart` and `UserPromptSubmit` hook entries include a `commandWindows` field alongside `command`:

```json
{
  "type": "command",
  "command": "node \"${PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/ponytail-activate.js\"",
  "commandWindows": "node \"%PLUGIN_ROOT%\\hooks\\ponytail-activate.js\"",
  "timeout": 5,
  "statusMessage": "Loading ponytail mode..."
}
```

`commandWindows` doesn't appear to be part of the currently supported hooks schema on Claude.ai's marketplace validator, so it rejects the whole plugin as `failed_content` ‚Äî even though it presumably works fine locally in Claude Code.

Could the Windows-specific command be handled another way (e.g. a single cross-platform `command`, or a wrapper script) so the plugin validates on Claude.ai's marketplace too? Happy to retest once updated. Thanks for the plugin!

title: Add oh-my-pi support
author: CyJaySong

title: [Bug] /ponytail never sets a mode in Claude Code: the UserPromptSubmit hook receives the SKILL.md body, not the command text
author: Zoz92
body:
# [Bug] `/ponytail` never sets a mode in Claude Code: the UserPromptSubmit hook receives the SKILL.md body, not the command text

## Summary

In Claude Code, `/ponytail` is dispatched as a **skill**, not as a `commands/*.md` slash command. The harness therefore hands `UserPromptSubmit` a `data.prompt` that begins with `<command-message>` / `<command-name>` tags and then contains **the entire body of `skills/ponytail/SKILL.md`** ‚Äî not the literal string `/ponytail:ponytail`.

`hooks/ponytail-mode-tracker.js:23` tests:

```js
if (/^[/@$]ponytail/.test(prompt)) {
```

That anchor never matches, so `setMode()` is never called, `~/.claude/.ponytail-active` is never written, and everything that reads it (`ponytail-instructions.js`, `ponytail-subagent.js`, the statusline) sees no active mode.

This is the same root cause as the (now fixed) #161, seen from the other side. Before #162 landed, the old deactivation regex

```js
if (/\b(stop ponytail|normal mode)\b/i.test(prompt)) {
```

matched ponytail's **own** SKILL.md, which says on line 29:

> `unsure. Off only: "stop ponytail" / "normal mode". Default: **full**.`

So invoking `/ponytail:ponytail` printed `PONYTAIL MODE OFF`. The skill disabled itself by reading itself. #162's `isDeactivationCommand` fixed that half; the command-detection half is still open.

## Environment

- ponytail **4.8.4** (marketplace `DietrichGebert/ponytail`, user scope)
- Claude Code CLI **2.1.206**, Linux
- Hook wired via `hooks/claude-codex-hooks.json` ‚Üí `UserPromptSubmit` ‚Üí `ponytail-mode-tracker.js`

## Reproduction

1. Install ponytail 4.8.4 in Claude Code, start a session.
2. Invoke `/ponytail:ponytail` (or `/ponytail full`) from the slash menu.
3. Observe: no `PONYTAIL MODE CHANGED` output from the hook.
4. `ls ~/.claude/.ponytail-active` ‚Üí **No such file or directory**.

On 4.2.0, step 3 instead prints `PONYTAIL MODE OFF`, every single time.

Typing `ponytail ultra` as ordinary prose still works, because then `data.prompt` really is the literal text. Only the slash/skill path is affected ‚Äî which is the documented way to use it.

## Why this wasn't caught

#269 reported the `commands/*.toml` vs `commands/*.md` mismatch and was closed unmerged, with:

> the `UserPromptSubmit` hook (`ponytail-mode-tracker.js`) parses the prompt text, so `/ponytail ultra` persists the mode whether it came from a skill or was typed raw. It even handles the `/ponytail:ponytail` form.

The code does handle that form. The harness just never delivers it: when the command comes from a skill, the prompt is the skill body. The skills *do* show up in the `/` menu with their arg hints, as you observed ‚Äî that part is orthogonal, and it's what makes the failure invisible. The menu entry works; the hook behind it doesn't.

## Suggested fix

Prefer the `<command-name>` tag when present, and fall back to the raw prompt otherwise. Claude Code emits a `<command-args>` tag for at least some commands (empty when there is no argument); the snippet below tolerates its absence:

```js
const raw = data.prompt || '';
const name = raw.match(/<command-name>\s*([^<]+?)\s*<\/command-name>/)?.[1];
const args = raw.match(/<command-args>\s*([^<]*?)\s*<\/command-args>/)?.[1];
const prompt = (name ? `${name} ${args ?? ''}` : raw).trim().toLowerCase();
```

Everything downstream (`^[/@$]ponytail`, the `parts[0]` / `parts[1]` split, `isDeactivationCommand`) then works unchanged, and `isDeactivationCommand` keeps protecting the raw-prose path.

Worth a regression test that feeds the tracker a `data.prompt` built from the real skill body: it fails on both counts today (no activation; and on any build predating #162, a spurious deactivation).

title: Marketplace sync fails in Claude Desktop (Cowork) due to unresolvable $schema URL
author: benjemaaGit
body:
**Description**

Adding this repo as a plugin marketplace in Claude Desktop (Cowork's "Add marketplace" / "Add from repository" flow) fails with:

> Marketplace sync failed. Check the repository URL and try again.

This happens both with the full GitHub URL (https://github.com/DietrichGebert/ponytail) and the short form (DietrichGebert/ponytail), and persists after restarting the app.

**Suspected cause**

.claude-plugin/marketplace.json declares:

"$schema": "https://anthropic.com/claude-code/marketplace.schema.json"

That URL currently returns nothing/404 ‚Äî it doesn't resolve to a real schema document. If Claude Desktop's marketplace importer tries to fetch and validate against the declared $schema and fails when the URL is unreachable, that would explain the sync failure.

**Evidence supporting this theory**

- The Claude Code CLI (`/plugin marketplace add DietrichGebert/ponytail`) installs this marketplace without issue ‚Äî the CLI likely ignores $schema.
- Other public marketplace repos without a $schema field (e.g. mrlm-xyz/demo-claude-marketplace, rohitg00/agentmemory) sync successfully in Claude Desktop using the exact same "Add from repository" flow.
- Ponytail's manifest is the only one tested that includes a $schema field, and it's the only one that fails.

**Suggested fix**

Remove the $schema field from .claude-plugin/marketplace.json, or point it to a URL that actually resolves to a valid schema document.

**Environment**

- Claude Desktop (Cowork), current version as of July 2026
- Repo tested: DietrichGebert/ponytail, main branch

title: Codex hooks fail JSON validation after #508
author: saime428
body:
## Environment

- Ponytail `main` at `523e9dc` (4.8.4)
- Codex CLI 0.144.0
- Windows 10
- Regression: #508 / `33c00d3`

## Bug

After #508, Ponytail emits Codex `additionalContext` at the top level. Codex rejects it before context injection:

```text
SessionStart hook (failed)
  error: hook returned invalid session start JSON output
```

Current output:

```json
{"systemMessage":"PONYTAIL:FULL","additionalContext":"PONYTAIL MODE ACTIVE ..."}
```

Required output:

```json
{"systemMessage":"PONYTAIL:FULL","hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"PONYTAIL MODE ACTIVE ..."}}
```

A deterministic check against current `main` fails because the output has a top-level `additionalContext` and no `hookSpecificOutput`.

## Evidence

The official Codex 0.144.0 schemas set root `additionalProperties: false` and only define `additionalContext` inside `hookSpecificOutput`:

- [SessionStart](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/rust-v0.144.0/codex-rs/hooks/schema/generated/session-start.command.output.schema.json)
- [UserPromptSubmit](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/rust-v0.144.0/codex-rs/hooks/schema/generated/user-prompt-submit.command.output.schema.json)
- [SubagentStart](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/rust-v0.144.0/codex-rs/hooks/schema/generated/subagent-start.command.output.schema.json)

The [0.142.5 SessionStart schema](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/rust-v0.142.5/codex-rs/hooks/schema/generated/session-start.command.output.schema.json) has the same shape, so this is distinct from #505.

## Root cause and fix

#508 flattened the shared Codex branch of `writeHookOutput`. That affects SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, and SubagentStart whenever context is present.

Restore the event-specific `hookSpecificOutput` wrapper and update the existing tests to assert the matching `hookEventName`, nested `additionalContext`, and no top-level field.

title: ###Bug Hooks defined in `hooks/claude-codex-hooks.json` (SessionStart, SubagentStart, UserPromptSubmit)
author: Gentleman999s
body: ### Environment - OS: Windows 11 - Terminal: Git Bash (MinTTY,Windows Terminal with GitBash ) - ponytail version: 4.8.4 - Claude Code CLI On Windows, when running Claude Code from Git Bash, the `command` (bash) variant appears to be selected/executed via PowerShell instead of `commandWindows`, producing: UserPromptSubmit hook error Failed with non-blocking status code: exec : The term 'exec' is not recognized as the name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or operable program. `exec` is a bash builtin and is not valid PowerShell syntax, hence the failure. The error is non-blocking, but it fires on every prompt submission and pollutes output. ### Expected On Windows (regardless of which terminal/shell the user is interacting in), the `commandWindows` variant should be selected so `ponytail-mode-tracker.js` etc. run correctly. ### Repro 1. Install ponytail plugin on Windows. 2. Open Claude Code CLI via Git Bash. 3. Submit any prompt. 4. Observe the hook error above.

title: [Bug]Indexing crashes on non-ASCII (CJK) repository paths on Windows
author: squall-cyber

title: [Bug] Unhandled fs error when writing default mode in pi-extension
author: guyoron1
body:
Heyaaaaa all : )

The `/ponytail default <mode>` handler in `pi-extension/index.js` calls `writeDefaultMode()` without any error handling. Under the hood that function does `fs.mkdirSync()` + `fs.writeFileSync()` ‚Äî both of which can throw if permissions are wrong, disk is full, etc.

Since the handler is `async`, the thrown error becomes an unhandled promise rejection and the user gets zero feedback about what went wrong. They just... set a default and nothing happens.

Wrapping it in a try-catch with a `ctx?.ui?.notify?.()` on failure should do the trick. Quick fix, much better UX when things go sideways.

title: [Bug] Review mode has no icon in pi-extension status bar
author: guyoron1
body:
Heyaaaaa all : )

Small UX thing I noticed ‚Äî when you switch to `review` mode, the status bar shows a blank where the icon should be:

```
‚óè üê¥ ponytail:  REVIEW
```

Meanwhile `lite` gets üåø, `full` gets ‚ö°, `ultra` gets üî• ‚Äî but `review` was left out of the `levelIcons` map even though it's in `VALID_MODES`. Just needs one more entry:

```js
const levelIcons = { lite: "üåø", full: "‚ö°", ultra: "üî•", review: "üìã" };
```

Nothing breaking, just looks a bit off without it.

title: [Bug] OpenCode plugin crashes on non-string input.arguments (type coercion)
author: guyoron1
body:
Heyaaaaa all : )

Found a sneaky one in the OpenCode plugin ‚Äî `ponytail/.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs:95` does this:

```js
const mode = normalizePersistedMode((input.arguments || '').trim()) || getDefaultMode();
```

The thing is, if `input.arguments` happens to be a non-string truthy value (say a number), the `||` doesn't kick in and `.trim()` blows up with a `TypeError`. Classic JS gotcha.

Funny enough, the pi-extension already handles this correctly at `index.js:90` with `String(args || "").trim()` ‚Äî so the fix is literally just matching what's already done elsewhere in the codebase.

**Fix:**
```js
const mode = normalizePersistedMode(String(input.arguments || '').trim()) || getDefaultMode();
```

One-liner, but it prevents a full hook crash.

title: ponytail-mcp: McpServer version hardcoded to 0.1.0 instead of reading package.json
author: guyoron1
body:
**Bug**

`ponytail-mcp/index.js:12` passes `version: "0.1.0"` to the `McpServer` constructor:

```javascript
const server = new McpServer({ name: "ponytail", version: "0.1.0" });
```

However, `ponytail-mcp/package.json` declares `"version": "4.8.4"`. MCP clients that inspect the server version see a stale `0.1.0`, and this will need manual updating on every release ‚Äî something easy to forget.

**Fix**

Read the version from `package.json` at startup:

```javascript
import { readFileSync } from 'fs';
const { version } = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL('./package.json', import.meta.url), 'utf8'));
const server = new McpServer({ name: "ponytail", version });
```

Or use a `createRequire`-based import if consistency with the other adapters matters.

title: hooks: writeDefaultMode BOM strip uses literal character instead of Ôªø escape
author: guyoron1
body:
**Bug**

`hooks/ponytail-config.js` line 107 strips the UTF-8 BOM using a **literal BOM character** in the regex, while every other BOM strip in the codebase uses the Unicode escape `Ôªø`:

- `ponytail-config.js:87` ‚Äî `Ôªø`
- `ponytail-activate.js:49` ‚Äî `Ôªø`
- `ponytail-mode-tracker.js:16` ‚Äî `Ôªø`
- `scripts/uninstall.js:26` ‚Äî `Ôªø`

If the source file is re-encoded or processed by a tool that strips invisible characters, the literal BOM match silently breaks, while the `Ôªø` escape is encoding-safe.

**Fix**
Change line 107 to use the `Ôªø` escape, consistent with the rest of the codebase.

title: benchmarks: behavior.js codeOf() misses CRLF code fences (companion to #339)
author: guyoron1
body:
**Bug**

`benchmarks/behavior.js` line 14 uses `/```[\w-]*\n/` to match fenced code blocks. The `\n` literal doesn't match `\r\n` (CRLF), so on Windows the regex fails to extract code from fences ‚Äî causing `codeOf()` to return empty and the behavior gate to grade on raw text.

This is the same class of bug as #339 (which covers `loc.js`), but `behavior.js` was not mentioned there and is not addressed by PR #518.

**Steps to reproduce**
```js
// LF works:
codeOf('```python\ndef f(): pass\n```');  // ‚Üí "def f(): pass\n"
// CRLF fails:
codeOf('```python\r\ndef f(): pass\r\n```');  // ‚Üí "" (empty)
```

**Fix**
Change line 14 from `\n` to `\r?\n` in the fence regex.

title: Statusline setup nudge leaks into VS Code Copilot (`isCopilot` misses `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT`)
author: wdw89
body:
## Statusline setup nudge leaks into VS Code Copilot (`isCopilot` misses `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT`)

### Bug

`ponytail-activate.js:45` guards the statusline nudge with `!isCodex && !isCopilot`. But `ponytail-runtime.js:6` detects Copilot via:

```js
const isCopilot = Boolean(process.env.COPILOT_PLUGIN_DATA);
```

VS Code Copilot **never sets** `COPILOT_PLUGIN_DATA`; it only injects `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` (confirmed from a live SessionStart hook env on Windows, VS Code Copilot, ponytail 4.8.4). So `isCopilot=false` ‚Üí ponytail assumes native Claude Code CLI ‚Üí emits the `STATUSLINE SETUP NEEDED` nudge telling the user to edit `~/.claude/settings.json`'s `statusLine` ‚Äî a key VS Code Copilot doesn't read. Pure noise.

### Reproduce

1. Install ponytail into `~/.vscode/agent-plugins/github.com/DietrichGebert/ponytail`.
2. Don't add a `statusLine` key to `~/.claude/settings.json`.
3. Start a new Copilot chat ‚Üí SessionStart hook `Output:` ends with `STATUSLINE SETUP NEEDED: ...`.

### Suggested fix

Detect VS Code Copilot from `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` as a fallback when `COPILOT_PLUGIN_DATA` is unset:

```js
const isCopilot = Boolean(process.env.COPILOT_PLUGIN_DATA) ||
  (process.env.CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT || '')
    .split(/[\\/]+/).includes('agent-plugins') &&
  (process.env.CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT || '').toLowerCase().includes('.vscode');
```

Also guard `stateDir` ‚Äî the `isCopilot` branch reads `process.env.COPILOT_PLUGIN_DATA` which is unset under VS Code, so fall back to `getClaudeDir()`:

```js
let stateDir = getClaudeDir();
if (isCodex) stateDir = process.env.PLUGIN_DATA;
if (process.env.COPILOT_PLUGIN_DATA) stateDir = process.env.COPILOT_PLUGIN_DATA;
```

### Workaround

Add any `statusLine` entry to `~/.claude/settings.json`. The check at `ponytail-activate.js:51` only verifies presence, so a stub value silences the nudge (VS Code Copilot ignores the file).

### Env

ponytail 4.8.4 ¬∑ VS Code Copilot (agent-plugins install) ¬∑ Windows 11

title: VS Code Copilot: hooks fail on Windows/PowerShell (`exec` is a bash builtin, not recognized by PowerShell)
author: wdw89
body:
## Bug

When ponytail is installed as a **VS Code Copilot** agent plugin on Windows, every hook (`SessionStart`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `SubagentStart`) fails with:

```
exec : The term 'exec' is not recognized as a cmdlet
+ CategoryInfo : ObjectNotFound: (exec:String) [], CommandNotFoundException
```

## Cause (3-step chain)

1. **VS Code Copilot reads `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`, NOT `.github/plugin/plugin.json`.** Grepping Copilot's bundled `cli.js` confirms the manifest path is hardcoded:

   ```js
   let Y = [uz(q, ".claude-plugin", "plugin.json"), ...z];
   ```

   `.github/plugin/plugin.json` (which correctly points to `copilot-hooks.json` with separate `bash`/`powershell` variants) is never read by Copilot.

2. **Therefore Copilot loads `hooks/claude-codex-hooks.json`** (the file `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` points to), not `hooks/copilot-hooks.json`.

3. **On Windows, Copilot executes the `command` field via PowerShell, not bash.** `claude-codex-hooks.json` uses `exec node "..."`:

   ```json
   "command": "exec node \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/ponytail-mode-tracker.js\""
   ```

   `exec` is a **bash/POSIX builtin** (process replacement). It does not exist in PowerShell ‚Üí `CommandNotFoundException` on the very first token.

## Why Claude Code is unaffected

Claude Code on Windows dispatches via the `commandWindows` field (which uses a PowerShell-native `if (Get-Command node...)` guard, no `exec`). VS Code Copilot uses the `command` field regardless of OS.

## Fix: use a shell-agnostic command

Replace `exec node "..."` with `node "..."` in all 3 `command` fields. This single form runs natively in **both** bash and PowerShell:

```diff
- "command": "exec node \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/ponytail-activate.js\"",
+ "command": "node \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/ponytail-activate.js\"",
```

(same for `ponytail-subagent.js` and `ponytail-mode-tracker.js`)

`exec` is a micro-optimization (replaces the shell process with node) that is irrelevant for one-shot hooks. Without it, bash spawns node as a child ‚Äî identical behavior; PowerShell runs it natively. Zero side effects on Claude Code / Codex.

## Env

- Windows 11, VS Code 1.127.0, Copilot v0.55.0, ponytail 4.8.4, node v22.23.1

title: SubagentStart hook has no matcher ‚Äî allow scoping the persona injection to specific agent types
author: dividedby
body:
## Request

Add an **agent-type matcher (or opt-in scoping config)** to the plugin's `SubagentStart` hook so consumers can limit the persona injection to specific subagent types.

## Why

The `SubagentStart` hook currently ships with no matcher, so its ~1,300-token persona is injected into **every** subagent spawned via the Agent tool ‚Äî including cheap read-only agents (search/verify) and low-tier agents whose entire base prompt is ~150‚Äì200 words. On those it's a 5‚Äì6√ó prompt tax and, for agents that never write code, dead weight.

Claude Code `SubagentStart` hooks support a `matcher` that filters by agent type name, and the hook receives `agent_type` on stdin ‚Äî so scoping is technically possible. The problem is it can only be done **on the hook's own definition**: a consumer cannot suppress or narrow a plugin-provided hook from their own `settings.json` (plugin hooks merge with user/project hooks; both fire). So the only place this can be fixed is upstream, here.

## Proposed options (any one solves it)

1. Add a default `matcher` on the SubagentStart hook that targets code-writing agents, or
2. Make the matcher configurable via plugin settings (e.g. an `agents` allowlist), or
3. Have the hook self-filter on the `agent_type` it receives on stdin against a documented default/env-overridable list.

Opt-in scoping (off by default = current behavior) would be the least disruptive.

## Context

Filed from a downstream config repo where this surfaced in a per-call overhead audit. Happy to send a PR if you point me at the preferred approach.

title: Bug: SessionStart Hook Output Not Injected Into Agent Context
author: chmichae1
body:
## Environment

- **Codex CLI Version:** 0.142.5
- **OS:** Ubuntu (WSL2)
- **Plugin:** ponytail@ponytail v4.8.4

## Summary

SessionStart hooks execute and produce correct JSON output, but the `additionalContext` is not injected into the agent's system prompt. The agent does not receive hook-provided instructions.

## Expected Behavior

Hook returns JSON with `additionalContext`:
```json
{
  "systemMessage": "PONYTAIL:FULL",
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "SessionStart",
    "additionalContext": "PONYTAIL MODE ACTIVE ‚Äî level: full\n\n[...instructions...]"
  }
}
```

Agent should receive and follow these instructions.

## Actual Behavior

1. ‚úÖ Hook configured and trusted in `~/.codex/config.toml`
2. ‚úÖ Hook executes successfully (verified manually)
3. ‚úÖ Mode flag file created correctly
4. ‚ùå **Agent receives no ponytail instructions**

## Reproduction

1. Install and enable ponytail plugin
2. Trust SessionStart hook
3. Start new Codex CLI session
4. Ask agent if it received ponytail instructions ‚Üí "no"

## Manual Test

```bash
PLUGIN_DATA=~/.codex/plugins/data/ponytail-ponytail \
CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT=~/.codex/plugins/cache/ponytail/ponytail/4.8.4 \
node ~/.codex/plugins/cache/ponytail/ponytail/4.8.4/hooks/ponytail-activate.js
```

Output: Correct JSON with `systemMessage` and `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext`

## Hook Implementation

```javascript
if (isCodex) {
  const output = { systemMessage: `PONYTAIL:${mode.toUpperCase()}` };
  if (context) {
    output.hookSpecificOutput = {
      hookEventName: event,
      additionalContext: context,
    };
  }
  process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(output));
}
```

## Workaround

Create `~/AGENTS.md` with desired instructions. Codex CLI loads AGENTS.md from workspace root.

title: SubagentStart hook injects lazy-mode into every subagent, including code reviewers; uninstall clobbers combined statusLine
author: jra2s394
body:
Two issues in v4.8.3.

**1. Unmatched SubagentStart injection biases review agents.** `hooks/claude-codex-hooks.json` registers the SubagentStart hook with no matcher, so the full lazy-dev ruleset ("at most three short lines", "no essays", minimize output) is injected into every subagent, including code-review, audit, and security agents. A reviewer told to keep output to three short lines reports fewer findings. Confirmed live: audit subagents spawned from a session with ponytail installed each received `PONYTAIL MODE ACTIVE` in their context. Suggested fix: gate the injection on agent type, or expose a config flag to exclude review-class subagents, or at minimum document how to remove the SubagentStart block.

**2. `scripts/uninstall.js` (line 33) deletes the whole statusLine setting on a substring match.** If the user has a combined statusline command that merely contains `ponytail-statusline`, uninstall removes the entire `statusLine` key, destroying the parts that belong to other tools. Suggested fix: strip only the ponytail segment from the command string and keep the key when other content remains.

title: bug: Copilot CLI reports 6 duplicate skill failures from .openclaw/skills/
author: kruzyk
body:
## Title
bug: Copilot CLI reports 6 duplicate skill failures from .openclaw/skills/
## Body
### Problem
When ponytail is installed as a **GitHub Copilot CLI** plugin, the CLI reports 6 skill loading failures at every startup:
```
Failed to load 6 skills. Run /skills for more details
```
**Root cause:** Copilot CLI recursively discovers every `SKILL.md` file inside the installed plugin directory. It finds skills in both `skills/` and `.openclaw/skills/`, which have identical `name:` values ‚Äî 6 collisions.
```
FAIL: ponytail         (.openclaw/skills/ponytail        collides with skills/ponytail)
FAIL: ponytail-audit    (.openclaw/skills/ponytail-audit   collides with skills/ponytail-audit)
FAIL: ponytail-debt     (.openclaw/skills/ponytail-debt    collides with skills/ponytail-debt)
FAIL: ponytail-gain     (.openclaw/skills/ponytail-gain    collides with skills/ponytail-gain)
FAIL: ponytail-help     (.openclaw/skills/ponytail-help    collides with skills/ponytail-help)
FAIL: ponytail-review   (.openclaw/skills/ponytail-review  collides with skills/ponytail-review)
```
The plugin correctly declares `"skills": "skills/"` in `.github/plugin/plugin.json`, but the CLI ignores that manifest and scans all directories.
### Analysis
`.openclaw/skills/` has shorter, OpenClaw-format descriptions and extra frontmatter fields (`homepage`, `license`). It is clearly intended for a different tool and should not be loaded by Copilot CLI.
The `package.json` `"files"` field does **not** include `.openclaw/`, so npm packages exclude it correctly. However, Copilot CLI installs via `copilot plugin marketplace add` + `copilot plugin install`, which clones the full repo ‚Äî `.openclaw/` ends up on disk even though it is not declared as a Copilot CLI asset.
### Why this matters
Every Copilot CLI startup shows a noisy failure banner. Users who install ponytail alongside other multi-tool plugins can hit 20+ phantom failures, making it impossible to tell whether a real loading error exists.
### Proposed solutions
Any of these would fix it from the plugin side:
| # | Approach | Effort | Side-effects |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Deduplicate**: drop `.openclaw/skills/` entirely and point OpenClaw at the existing `skills/` directory. Both use SKILL.md format; differences are just description length + 2 extra frontmatter keys (`homepage`, `license`). | Low | OpenClaw gets the longer descriptions ‚Äî arguably better |
| 2 | **Rename `.openclaw/skills/*/SKILL.md`** to a name OpenClaw supports but Copilot CLI does not scan (e.g. `skill.md` lowercase). | Low | Only works if OpenClaw accepts alternative filenames |
| 3 | **Exclude `.openclaw/` from the git-cloned copy** that Copilot CLI installs. Use `.gitattributes export-ignore` so `git archive` (and tools that use it) skip `.openclaw/`. | Medium | Depends on how Copilot CLI clones the repo |

Option 1 is simplest and removes the maintenance burden of keeping two directories in sync.
From the **Copilot CLI side**, the CLI should respect `.github/plugin/plugin.json` manifests and only scan declared directories. This is arguably a CLI bug, but the workaround above is fully in the plugin author's control.
### Repro
```bash
copilot plugin marketplace add DietrichGebert/ponytail
copilot plugin install ponytail@ponytail
copilot   # ‚Üí "Failed to load 6 skills"
```
### Environment
- **Copilot CLI** latest
- **ponytail** v4.8.4
- macOS (also likely affects Linux)
- `.github/plugin/plugin.json` present with `"skills": "skills/"`
